headline: Ghosts of the golden age of the Mediterranean
summary: A cache of colourful photos from the Magnum agency reveal a world where the beautiful and the everyday commingled
body_text:
Magnum is currently undertaking a fascinating project, a project so vast that it must seem Sisyphean to those working on it. Called simply A World in Color, the photographic agency has embarked on the digitisation of its Paris colour archives.

“Dormant for decades” is the way Magnum refers to its holdings of 650,000 colour slides. Now roused from its lengthy slumber with the support of Fujifilm, this vast cache of imagery is yielding some real treasure: every month, Magnum releases a curated, themed portfolio of photographs. And in the dark of long winter nights, A World in Color: The Mediterranean Edition, works like a virtual shot of vitamin D.

This selection demonstrates beyond doubt that there are as many Mediterraneans as there are eyes to see it, cameras to photograph it and minds to imagine it. The allure of Mare Nostrum, our sea, as the Romans called it, endures because it is an idea as much as a destination. It is a palimpsest of the fantasies of successive generations, whet
